aliases: 玉米片配芝士酱 (Yùmǐ Piàn Pèi Zhīshì Jiàng)

chinese: 玉米片配芝士酱 (Yùmǐ Piàn Pèi Zhīshì Jiàng)

cuisine: Tex-Mex / Mexican-American — adapted as Singapore cinema and casual café snack
region_of_origin: Mexican-American; widely served in Singapore cinemas and casual dining restaurants
food_subgroup: Commercially produced corn tortilla chips (lightly salted) with warm processed cheddar-style cheese sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no dedicated concept for nachos with processed cheese dip. Closest parent is a corn chip / tortilla chip snack food concept. No exact URI assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Nachos with processed cheese sauce — corn tortilla chips with warm cheese dip (Singapore cinema/café); FoodEx2 A0CZC used as closest parent for corn-based snack foods with added sauce
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code exists for nachos with cheese dip as a composite snack. A0CZC (corn-based snack) used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for nachos with cheese dip as a food item.

preparation_method: warm processed cheese sauce (heated emulsified cheese product)

serving.description: 1 cinema/café serving = 90g chips + 60g cheese sauce = 150g total
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cinema or café serving of nachos with cheese dip is approximately 150g, comprising roughly 90g of corn tortilla chips and 60g of warm processed cheese sauce, as typically served at Golden Village, Cathay cinemas, TGI Fridays, and Chili's Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Nachos with Cheese Dip — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for nachos with cheese sauce. Values represent a composite of corn tortilla chips (approximately 60% by weight) and processed cheddar-style cheese sauce (approximately 40% by weight), consistent with the standard cinema/café serving ratio. All per-100g values are derived from this composite ratio as reported in USDA SR Legacy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Corn Tortilla Chips and Nachos with Cheese — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 73 (high) is referenced from the University of Sydney GI Database for corn tortilla chips and nachos with cheese, which report GI values in the range of 70–78. The composite nachos-with-cheese-sauce product is estimated at GI 73 based on the dominant corn chip starch fraction (approximately 60% of total weight). Glycemic load per 150g serving calculated as: (73 × 44.5g carbohydrate per 100g × 150g serving / 100) / 100 = 48.6 GL units. The cheese sauce protein and fat content moderates the GI slightly from the chip-only value.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content at 822mg per 100g from salted corn chips and processed cheese emulsifying salts (sodium phosphate); a standard 150g cinema serving delivers approximately 1,233mg sodium — exceeding half the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000mg from a single snack.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid nachos with cheese dip entirely; even a half-serving (75g) delivers over 600mg sodium, and the processed cheese sauce contains sodium phosphate emulsifying salts that compound the sodium load beyond what is visible from chip salt alone.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 73) combined with high carbohydrate content (44.5g/100g) produces a glycemic load of 48.6 per standard 150g serving — equivalent to the glycemic impact of a large plate of white rice — driven primarily by the rapidly digestible starch in corn tortilla chips.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit nachos with cheese dip; if consumed, portion size should be reduced to no more than 50–75g total, chips should be dipped individually rather than smothered in cheese sauce, and the meal should be paired with a high-fibre food to blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (8.5g/100g) from processed cheddar-style cheese sauce, trans fat (0.2g/100g), very high sodium (822mg/100g), and high caloric density (398 kcal/100g) collectively elevate LDL cholesterol, blood pressure, and cardiovascular risk; cholesterol content is 25mg/100g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid nachos with cheese dip; the combination of saturated fat from processed cheese, trans fat, and critically high sodium creates a multiply-adverse cardiovascular risk profile that is not mitigated by the modest protein content or small amounts of micronutrients present.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (palmitic acid in processed cheese is proinflammatory), very high sodium (drives fluid retention and hypertension complications in RA), trans fat (0.2g/100g), and high GI (glycaemic inflammation pathway).
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid nachos with cheese dip; the combination of high GI, high saturated fat from processed cheese, trans fat, and very high sodium creates a multiply-proinflammatory snack with no redeeming anti-inflammatory properties.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nachos with Cheese Dip is a Tex-Mex-origin cinema and casual café snack that has become a staple at Golden Village and Cathay cinema concessions, TGI Fridays, Chili's, and food court American-style stalls across Singapore. The dish consists of crunchy, lightly salted deep-fried corn tortilla chips served alongside or topped with a warm, smooth, flowing processed cheddar-style cheese sauce — the iconic 'nacho cheese' that is either poured liberally over the chips or served as a side dip. The chips are golden-yellow, crisp, and triangular, with a mild corn flavour and a satisfying crunch; the cheese sauce is bright orange, viscous, salty, and savoury with a mild cheddar tang. Nutritionally, nachos with cheese dip is one of the most calorie-dense and sodium-heavy snack foods available in Singapore food service: 398 kcal per 100g, 822mg sodium per 100g (among the highest of any snack food), 21.5g fat per 100g, and 44.5g carbohydrate per 100g. The processed cheese sauce uses sodium phosphate emulsifying salts to maintain its smooth, pourable texture when heated — these emulsifying salts are the primary sodium carrier, as processed cheese contains approximately 3–4 times the sodium of natural cheese per gram. A standard 150g cinema serving (90g chips + 60g cheese sauce) delivers approximately 597 kcal, 1,233mg sodium (over half the WHO daily limit), and a glycemic load of 48.6 — comparable to a large plate of white rice. The GI is high at 73, driven by the rapidly digestible corn starch in the chips. Clinically, this food is unsuitable for patients with hypertension, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is vegetarian and generally considered halal in Singapore cinema and café contexts.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for nachos with cheese sauce — an approved primary source with direct coverage of this composite food. GI referenced from the University of Sydney GI Database for corn tortilla chips and nachos with cheese (GI 70–78 range). All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are populated from the approved source. Confidence is high as the USDA SR Legacy entry directly covers this food category.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for the nachos-with-cheese-sauce composite is not separately reported in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of the accessed date; individual fatty acid fractions for the processed cheese sauce component are not available at this level of detail. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite nachos-with-cheese-sauce product is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry consulted. All other micronutrients are populated from the USDA SR Legacy source. GI of 73 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database range of 70–78 for corn tortilla chips and nachos with cheese; the composite product GI is estimated at the midpoint of this range. Glycemic load of 48.6 per 150g serving calculated as (73 × 44.5 / 100) × 1.5 = 48.6. The description in the input JSON incorrectly states 'fresh tomato-based salsa dip' — this record covers the processed cheese sauce variant (cinema/café nachos), not a salsa variant; the description has been corrected accordingly. The alias 'Nachos with salsa' from the input has been retained as an alternate English name for retrieval purposes but is not the primary identity of this record.
